revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大山祇神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_imazato.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町今里郷232 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|57|02.2|N|129|03|01.6|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大山祇命、事代主命 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =1月23日 |神事 = 的射神事（1月8日） }}
'''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]今里（いまざと）郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[大山祇命]]、[[事代主命]]を主祭神に祀る。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。[[江戸時代]]中期に記された今里村の地図には当社が記されていることから、それ以前に創建されたものである。[[ファイル:IMG_imazato1.JPG|240px|thumb|社殿]]当初、郷内別地に石祠のみ奉祀されていたが、[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）に社殿を造営。しかし境内地が狭かったため、[[大正]]3年（[[1914年]]）9月に現在地に奉遷し、[[昭和]]27年（[[1952年]]）に社殿を新築した。
また、年代は不詳だが、郷内・蛭子神社を[[合祀]]している。
== 祭祀 == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
;主な祭礼・神事 *初祈祷（的射神事）（1月8日） *[[例大祭]]（1月23日）
== その他の神社 == 隣接する三日ノ浦郷に'''[[金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|金刀比羅神社]]'''が、道土井郷に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_mika.JPG|金刀比羅神社 画像: IMG_michidoi.JPG|大山祇神社</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 大山祇神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_imazato.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町今里郷232 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|57|02.2|N|129|03|01.6|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 大山祇命、事代主命 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =1月23日 |神事 = 的射神事（1月8日） }}
'''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]今里（いまざと）郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[大山祇命]]、[[事代主命]]を主祭神に祀る。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。[[江戸時代]]中期に記された今里村の地図には当社が記されていることから、それ以前に創建されたものである。[[ファイル:IMG_imazato1.JPG|240px|thumb|社殿]]当初、郷内別地に石祠のみ奉祀されていたが、[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）に社殿を造営。しかし境内地が狭かったため、[[大正]]3年（[[1914年]]）9月に現在地に奉遷し、[[昭和]]27年（[[1952年]]）に社殿を新築した。
また、年代は不詳だが、郷内・蛭子神社を[[合祀]]している。
== 祭祀 == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
;主な祭礼・神事 *初祈祷（的射神事）（1月8日） *[[例大祭]]（1月23日）
== その他の神社 == 隣接する三日ノ浦郷に'''[[金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|金刀比羅神社]]'''が、道土井郷に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_mika.JPG|金刀比羅神社 画像: IMG_michidoi.JPG|大山祇神社</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 平成3年 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 祭祀 == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・雄嶽日枝神社の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
;主な祭礼・神事 *初祈祷（的射神事）（[[1月8日]]） *[[例大祭]]（[[1月23日]]）
== その他の神社 == 隣接する三日ノ浦郷に'''[[金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|金刀比羅神社]]'''が、道土井郷に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_mika.JPG|金刀比羅神社 画像: IMG_michidoi.JPG|大山祇神社</gallery>

== 祭神 == [[大山祇命]]、[[事代主命]]を祀る。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。[[江戸時代]]中期に記された今里村の地図に当社が記されていることから、それ以前に創建されたものである。当初、郷内別地に石祠のみ奉祀し、[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）に新社殿を造営。しかし境内地が狭かったため、[[大正]]3年（[[1914年]]）9月に現在地に奉遷し、[[昭和]]27年（[[1952年]]）に社殿を新築した。 [[ファイル:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|社殿]]
明治7年（[[1874年]]）5月5日に[[無格社]]に列せられる。大正2年（[[1923年]]）10月3日に、郷内・蛭子神社を[[合祀]]した。
== 祭祀 == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・[[雄嶽日枝神社]]の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
;主な祭礼・神事 *初祈祷（的射神事）（[[1月8日]]） *[[例大祭]]（[[1月23日]]）
== その他の神社 == 隣接する三日ノ浦郷に'''[[金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|金刀比羅神社]]'''が、道土井郷に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_mika.JPG|金刀比羅神社 画像: IMG_michidoi.JPG|大山祇神社</gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 (1991年) *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部 {{神道 横}}
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]]

revision.text: == Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== 祭神 == The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** and **Kotoshironushi**.
== 歴史 == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. However, historical maps from the **mid-Edo period** indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a **stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district**. In **1883 (Meiji 16)**, a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was **relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)**. Later, in **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was officially classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)**. On **October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)**, the **local Ebisu Shrine** was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== 祭祀 == Although the shrine is located within **former Kamigotō Town**, it does not host **Kamigotō Kagura** performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the **priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō**, which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)** – January 8 * **Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)** – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)}}** – Located in **Mikano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}}** – Located in **Michidoi-gō**
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * **"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)** – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== 祭神 == The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** and **Kotoshironushi**.
== 歴史 == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. However, historical maps from the **mid-Edo period** indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a **stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district**. In **1883 (Meiji 16)**, a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was **relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)**. Later, in **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was officially classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)**. On **October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)**, the **local Ebisu Shrine** was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== 祭祀 == Although the shrine is located within **former Kamigotō Town**, it does not host **Kamigotō Kagura** performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the **priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō**, which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)** – January 8 * **Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)** – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)}}** – Located in **Mikano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}}** – Located in **Michidoi-gō**
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * **"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)** – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== 祭神 == The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** and **Kotoshironushi**.
== 歴史 == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. However, historical maps from the **mid-Edo period** indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a **stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district**. In **1883 (Meiji 16)**, a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was **relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)**. Later, in **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was officially classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)**. On **October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)**, the **local Ebisu Shrine** was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== 祭祀 == Although the shrine is located within **former Kamigotō Town**, it does not host **Kamigotō Kagura** performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the **priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō**, which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)** – January 8 * **Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)** – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)}}** – Located in **Mikano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}}** – Located in **Michidoi-gō**
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * **"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)** – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== 祭神 == The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** and **Kotoshironushi**.
== 歴史 == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. However, historical maps from the **mid-Edo period** indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a **stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district**. In **1883 (Meiji 16)**, a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was **relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)**. Later, in **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was officially classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)**. On **October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)**, the **local Ebisu Shrine** was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== 祭祀 == Although the shrine is located within **former Kamigotō Town**, it does not host **Kamigotō Kagura** performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the **priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō**, which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)** – January 8 * **Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)** – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)}}** – Located in **Mikano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}}** – Located in **Michidoi-gō**
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * **"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)** – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

*[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)}}[[Category:qq]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_imazato.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{coord|32|57|35.4|N|129|02|50.9|E|region:JP-42_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} |deities= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |shrine_rank= Former unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture**.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}} The shrine enshrines **Ōyamatsumi** and **Kotoshironushi**.
== 歴史 == The exact founding date of the shrine is **unknown**. However, historical maps from the **mid-Edo period** indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a **stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district**. In **1883 (Meiji 16)**, a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was **relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)**. Later, in **1952 (Shōwa 27)**, the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was officially classified as an **unranked shrine (mukakusha)**. On **October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)**, the **local Ebisu Shrine** was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== 祭祀 == Although the shrine is located within **former Kamigotō Town**, it does not host **Kamigotō Kagura** performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the **priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō**, which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * **First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)** – January 8 * **Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)** – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * **{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)}}** – Located in **Mikano-ura-gō** * **{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}}** – Located in **Michidoi-gō**
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * **"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)** – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * **"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)** – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== 祭祀 == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

[[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 4]]]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)}}[[Category:qq]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_imazato.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{coord|32|57|35.4|N|129|02|50.9|E|region:JP-42_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} |deities= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |shrine_rank= Former unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.

In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.

; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23

[[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)}}[[Category:11]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_imazato.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{coord|32|57|35.4|N|129|02|50.9|E|region:JP-42_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |shrine_rank= Former unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

[[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)}}[[Category:11]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_imazato.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{coord|32|57|35.4|N|129|02|50.9|E|region:JP-42_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |shrine_rank= Former unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

[[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)}}[[Category:11]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_imazato.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{coord|32|57|35.4|N|129|02|50.9|E|region:JP-42_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |shrine_rank= Former unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

[[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)}}[[Category:11]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:IMG_imazato.JPG|250px]] |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{coord|32|57|35.4|N|129|02|50.9|E|region:JP-42_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |shrine_rank= Former unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|12=draft}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=draft}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷) up to rev 98956134
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大山祇神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_imazato.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町今里郷232 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|57|35.4|N|129|02|50.9|E|region:JP-42_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=大山祇神社}} |祭神 = 大山祇命、事代主命 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =1月23日 |神事 = 的射神事（1月8日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=大山祇神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]今里郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。
== 祭神 == [[大山祇命]]、[[事代主命]]を祀る。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。[[江戸時代]]中期に記された今里村の地図に当社が記されていることから、それ以前に創建されたものである。当初、郷内別地に石祠のみ奉祀し、[[明治]]16年（[[1883年]]）に新社殿を造営。しかし境内地が狭かったため、[[大正]]3年（[[1914年]]）9月に現在地に奉遷し、[[昭和]]27年（[[1952年]]）に社殿を新築した。 [[ファイル:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|社殿]]
明治7年（[[1874年]]）5月5日に[[無格社]]に列せられる。大正2年（[[1923年]]）10月3日に、郷内・蛭子神社を[[合祀]]した。
== 祭祀 == 当社は旧[[上五島町]]内に鎮座するが、[[神楽]]を伝承していない荒川郷・[[雄嶽日枝神社]]の神主が受け持っている関係から[[上五島神楽]]の奉納はない。
;主な祭礼・神事 *初祈祷（的射神事）（[[1月8日]]） *[[例大祭]]（[[1月23日]]）
== その他の神社 == 隣接する三日ノ浦郷に'''[[金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|金刀比羅神社]]'''が、道土井郷に'''[[大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|大山祇神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像: IMG_mika.JPG|金刀比羅神社 画像: IMG_michidoi.JPG|大山祇神社</gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} *『[[上五島町]]郷土誌 平成16年』上五島町教育委員会 *『上五島町の文化遺産をたずねて』上五島町教育委員会 (1991年) *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部 {{神道 横}}
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]]

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.

; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23

Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.

== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11434673]] Wikidata name: Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * ]]]]]]{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG imazato.JPG |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.959833333333336|N|129.0474722222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|qq=|1=Ooyamazumi-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434672}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11434673]] Wikidata name: Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * ]]]]]]{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG imazato.JPG |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.959833333333336|N|129.0474722222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== その他の神社 == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|qq=|1=Ooyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434672}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11434673]] Wikidata name: Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * ]]]]]]{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG imazato.JPG |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.959833333333336|N|129.0474722222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|qq=|1=Ooyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434672}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11434673]] Wikidata name: Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * ]]]]]]{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG imazato.JPG |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.959833333333336|N|129.0474722222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|1=Ooyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434672}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11434673]] Wikidata name: Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * ]]]]]]{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG imazato.JPG |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.959833333333336|N|129.0474722222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|1=Ooyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434672}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|version=98956134|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11434673]] Wikidata name: Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * ]]]]]]{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG imazato.JPG |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.959833333333336|N|129.0474722222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|1=Ooyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434672}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|version=98956134|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|1=Ooyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434672}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11434673]] Wikidata name: Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * ]]]]]]{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG imazato.JPG |location= [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki]], Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.959833333333336|N|129.0474722222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|1=Ooyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434672}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11434673]] Wikidata name: Ooyamazumi-jinja (Imazato, Shinkamigoto) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * ]]]]]]{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG imazato.JPG |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki}}, Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.959833333333336|N|129.0474722222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|1=Ooyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434672}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}
