* ]]]]]]{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG imazato.JPG |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|1=Shin-Kamigotō}}, Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.959833333333336|N|129.0474722222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790|1=Shin-Kamigotō}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434672}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}

* ]]]]]]{{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= File:IMG imazato.JPG |location= {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki|1=Shin-Kamigotō}}, Imazato-gō 232 |coordinates= {{Coord|32.959833333333336|N|129.0474722222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |shrine_Rank= Former Unranked shrine (旧無格社) |established= Unknown |main_hall= |annual_festival= January 23 |ceremonies= Mato-ire Ritual (January 8) |map= {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Ōyamazumi Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture= {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790|1=Shin-Kamigotō}} }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in '''Imazato-gō, Shinkamigotō, Nagasaki Prefecture'''.
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The shrine enshrines '''Ōyamatsumi''' and '''Kotoshironushi'''.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is '''unknown'''. However, historical maps from the '''mid-Edo period''' indicate that the shrine already existed at that time.
Initially, the shrine was a '''stone sanctuary located at a different site within the district'''. In '''1883 (Meiji 16)''', a new shrine building was constructed, but the site was deemed too small. Consequently, the shrine was '''relocated to its current site in 1914 (Taishō 3)'''. Later, in '''1952 (Shōwa 27)''', the shrine building was reconstructed.
[[File:IMG_imazato1.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine building]]
In '''1874 (Meiji 7)''', the shrine was officially classified as an '''unranked shrine (mukakusha)'''. On '''October 3, 1923 (Taishō 2)''', the '''local Ebisu Shrine''' was merged into Ōyamazumi Shrine.
== Rites == Although the shrine is located within '''former Kamigotō Town''', it does not host '''Kamigotō Kagura''' performances. Instead, the shrine's rituals are conducted by the '''priest of Hie Shrine (雄嶽日枝神社) in Arakawa-gō''', which does not have a Kagura tradition.
; Major Festivals and Rituals * '''First Prayer Ceremony (Mato-ire Ritual)''' – January 8 * '''Annual Grand Festival (Reitaisai)''' – January 23
== Other Shrines == Nearby shrines include: * '''{{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (新上五島町三日ノ浦郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Mikano-ura-gō)|qq=}}''' – Located in '''Mikano-ura-gō''' * '''{{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shinkamigotō, Michidoi-gō)|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434672}}''' – Located in '''Michidoi-gō'''
<gallery> File:IMG_mika.JPG|Kotohira Shrine (Mikano-ura-gō) File:IMG_michidoi.JPG|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi-gō) </gallery>
== Bibliography == {{Commonscat}} * '''"Shinkamigotō Town History" (2004, Heisei 16)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Exploring the Cultural Heritage of Shinkamigotō Town" (1991, Heisei 3)''' – Shinkamigotō Town Board of Education * '''"Gotō Shrine Records" (1943, Shōwa 18, reprint edition)''' – Shingi-kai, Minamimatsuura District
{{Shinto-stub}}
